testName: should open shared skill via skill link and redirect through login page

fix.summary: After login, do not wait for a global mux-player. Wait for Lexical video wrappers (div[id^="component-"]) to appear, then let VideoPlayer.scrollToVideo handle IntersectionObserver-based mux mount.
fix.pattern: Replace post-login $('mux-player').waitForExist({ timeout: 120000 }) with browser.waitUntil(async () => (await $$('div[id^="component-"]')).length > 0, { timeout: 120000, interval: 1000, timeoutMsg: 'No video component wrappers found on skill page' }). Rely on test/pageobjects/adept.video.player.ts scrollToVideo for lazy mount of the targeted video.

investigationFindings: Git commit b65cff9 documents CI failures of mux-player not existing after 120s on this spec. Root cause: learn-webapp keeps only ~2-3 mux-player nodes in the DOM at any time; off-screen videos have no mux node until their wrapper is scrolled into view. Wrappers div[id^="component-"] are persistent and the correct readiness signal.
rootCauseChain: TIMING_ISSUE -> Lazy mux mount -> global mux wait never satisfied -> 120s timeout
